understanding.goal_understanding: Finish the current level (BP35) by navigating the player to the goal gem tile marked '+', avoiding spike tiles that immediately end the attempt, and doing so within the per-attempt turn limit and limited number of attempts.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each turn I could MOVE_LEFT or MOVE_RIGHT to shift one tile horizontally; after each horizontal move, gravity (in this episode, DOWN) automatically carried the player until landing on a solid/support tile, hitting a hazard (e.g., spikes 'u'/'v'), or reaching the goal '+'. CLICK could be used to remove clickable/breakable tiles at a specified [x,y] without moving the player and without consuming horizontal-move-related timers; this was used to open fall paths or change future landing outcomes. UNDO would revert the previous action to recover from mistakes. Planning relied on the state previews of left/right landing locations (and corridor scans when available) to choose safe moves that create useful vertical passages while avoiding hazards.

understanding.goal_understanding: Finish the current level (BP35) by navigating the player to the goal gem tile marked '+', avoiding spike tiles that immediately end the attempt, and doing so within the per-attempt turn limit and limited number of attempts.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each turn I could MOVE_LEFT or MOVE_RIGHT to shift one tile horizontally; after each horizontal move, gravity (in this episode, DOWN) automatically carried the player until landing on a solid/support tile, hitting a hazard (e.g., spikes 'u'/'v'), or reaching the goal '+'. CLICK could be used to remove clickable/breakable tiles at a specified [x,y] without moving the player and without consuming horizontal-move-related timers; this was used to open fall paths or change future landing outcomes. UNDO would revert the previous action to recover from mistakes. Planning relied on the state previews of left/right landing locations (and corridor scans when available) to choose safe moves that create useful vertical passages while avoiding hazards.
